word: G85 - ἀδημονέω - adēmonéō - ad-ay-mon-eh'-o - Be full of heaviness

sections.paragraph:
- Original: ἀδημονέω

- Transliteration: Ademoneo

- Phonetic: ad-ay-mon-eh'-o

- Definition:

1. to be troubled, great distress or anguish, depressed This is the strongest of the three Greek words (85, 916,

 inthe NT for depression.

- Origin: from a derivative of adeo (to be sated to loathing)

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Verb

- Strong's: From a derivative of ἀδέω adeō (to be sated to loathing); to be in distress (of mind): - be full of heaviness be very heavy.
